originalTitle: Sudan drone attack on key hospital killed 64 people during Eid, WHO says
title: Sad News from a Hospital in Sudan
content:
Hello, My Friends!

Hi everyone! It's your friendly news reporter, here to share something important with you today. We always talk about good things happening in Africa, like new schools or fun festivals. But sometimes, we hear news that makes us feel a little sad. Today, we have some sad news from a country called Sudan. Sudan is a big country in Africa, just like ours.

What Happened in Sudan?

Imagine a big hospital. This is a special place where doctors and nurses help people who are sick or hurt. They make everyone feel better and get healthy again. But in Sudan, something very sad happened at one of these important hospitals. A flying machine, called a drone, caused big trouble there. It was not a good drone that helps people; it was a drone that hurt people. This sad thing happened when people in Sudan were having a very special holiday. This holiday is called Eid. Eid is a time when families get together, share food, and celebrate. It's usually a very happy time for everyone to be safe and joyful. But for many people in Sudan, their Eid was filled with sadness because of what happened at the hospital. This should not happen, especially during a time of celebration when people expect to be safe.

What is a Hospital?

Did you know that hospitals are very important places? A hospital is like a giant house for helping people who are sick or have been hurt. When you have a tummy ache, or fall and scrape your knee badly, you might go to a hospital. Doctors and nurses work there day and night. They give medicine, fix broken bones, and make sure everyone gets healthy again. Think of doctors and nurses as superheroes in white coats! They are always trying to keep people safe and well. In hospitals, there are beds for sick people to rest quietly. There are also special machines that help doctors understand what is wrong inside someone's body. Hospitals are places of hope and healing. They are meant to be safe havens for everyone, especially for children who need care. Did you know? The very first hospitals were created a very long time ago, even before your great-grandparents were born! People have always wanted to help others who are sick or hurt.

A Special Day Called Eid

The sad news happened during Eid. Have you ever heard of Eid? It's a very important celebration for many people around the world, including in Africa. Families put on new clothes, visit friends and relatives, and eat delicious food together. Children often get gifts or special treats on Eid. It's a time for joy, laughter, and being thankful for family and friends. It's like Christmas or your birthday, but for many, many families at the same time! People want to feel safe and happy during Eid. So, it was extra sad that this bad thing happened when everyone hoped to be celebrating and feeling joyful. Holidays are meant for peace and happiness, not for trouble or sadness. It is very hard when a time for joy turns into a time of sorrow for so many people. Did you know? There are two main Eids celebrated each year: Eid al-Fitr and Eid al-Adha. Both are very special times for families to come together and celebrate peace and kindness.

Who Was Hurt?

This drone attack at the hospital hurt many people. The World Health Organization, which we call WHO, told us the sad numbers. WHO is like a big team of doctors and health helpers from all over the world. They try to make sure everyone is healthy and safe and that hospitals can help people. They said 64 people sadly lost their lives. That is a very big number. Imagine 64 of your friends from school; that's how many people were hurt badly. Among them were 13 children. Imagine little boys and girls, just like you, who were at the hospital trying to get better. This is very heartbreaking for everyone. Also, two nurses and a doctor who were working hard to help others were among those hurt. They were doing their job, trying to save lives, and they got hurt themselves. This makes us very, very sad. Hospitals should always be safe places for everyone. No one should ever hurt people in a hospital, especially those who are sick or the kind people who help them.

What is War?

Sometimes, in some countries, people fight. This fighting is called war. War is not like a small disagreement between friends or a little argument. War is when big groups of people or armies fight each other with weapons. It is very sad and makes many people afraid and causes a lot of damage. War can break things like homes and schools, and it can hurt innocent people who are not fighting. When there is war, hospitals become even more important. They are the places where people who get hurt in the fighting can go for help and healing. That is why it is so important that hospitals are safe from war. They are places for healing, not for fighting. We wish for war to stop, so everyone can live in peace and safety, especially children.

Who is WHO?

We mentioned WHO before. Let's talk a little more about them. WHO stands for the World Health Organization. They are like global health detectives and helpers. Their main job is to help all people everywhere in the world to be healthy. They share important information about how to stay well and prevent sickness. They also help countries when there are big health problems, like a new sickness spreading or when a hospital needs help after a disaster. They work very hard to make sure everyone has a chance to live a healthy life. When WHO speaks, they help us understand what is happening with people's health around the world. Did you know? WHO was started many, many years ago, after a big war, because people realized that health should be for everyone, everywhere, and that we need to work together to achieve it.

Why This Matters To Us

This news from Sudan makes us think. It reminds us that even when we are happy and safe, some people are facing difficult times. It's important to remember that all children, no matter where they live in Africa or the world, deserve to be safe and healthy. They deserve to play, learn, and grow without fear or sadness. We hope that the fighting in Sudan stops very, very soon. We hope that the hospitals can become safe places again for all the doctors, nurses, and especially the children who need their help. Everyone deserves peace and safety. We can all hope for peace and kindness to spread in the world. Let's send our kind thoughts to the children and families in Sudan. We wish them strength and hope. Let's remember that being kind and helping each other is the best way to make our world a better place for everyone. We want all of Africa, and the whole world, to be full of peace and happiness.
summary: Very sad news came from Sudan, where a hospital was attacked during a happy holiday called Eid, hurting many people, including children, doctors, and nurses.
whyMatters: This is important because hospitals should always be safe places for everyone, especially for sick children and the kind people who help them, and everyone deserves to live in peace without war.

originalTitle: Kenyans fighting illegally for Russia in Ukraine to be granted amnesty
title: Kenyans fighting illegally for Russia in Ukraine to be granted amnesty
content: It is illegal for Kenyans to serve in foreign armies and can carry a sentence of up to 10 years in jail.

originalTitle: 'Peace is a gradual thing': How land, cattle and identity fuel a deadly Nigerian conflict
title: Let's Learn About Peace in Nigeria!
content:
Hello, amazing friends! I'm your kid-friendly news reporter, and I'm here to tell you about something important happening in a wonderful country in Africa called Nigeria. Nigeria is a big, beautiful country with lots of sunshine and happy children, just like you! It is in West Africa.

What is Happening in Plateau State?

We are going to talk about a place in Nigeria called Plateau State. Imagine your school. Everyone wants to play nicely, right? Sometimes, in Plateau State, people who live very close to each other, like neighbours, have some tricky problems. These problems can make people sad, and we want to help them find their smiles again. We want everyone to be happy and peaceful, like good friends playing together. Did you know? Nigeria is home to over 200 million people, making it the most populated country in Africa! That's a lot of friends!

Land: Our Home and Our Food

Land is super important. It is the ground we walk on. It is where we build our homes. It is also where farmers grow delicious food like yams, maize, and rice. Imagine needing a piece of land to build your house or to plant your garden. Everyone needs a place to live and grow food. People want to be safe on their land. They want to know their homes are secure and their farms can grow food for their families. Sometimes, different groups of people need the same piece of land. Maybe one family wants to build a house there, and another family wants to plant their crops there. This can make things tricky. It's like two friends wanting to play with the same toy at the same time. What do good friends do? They try to share or take turns, right?

Cows: Important Friends!

In Nigeria, many people keep cows. Cows are wonderful animals! They give us milk to drink, and sometimes meat to eat. People who keep cows are often called herders. These herders need big, green fields for their cows to eat grass. Cows need a lot of space to wander and munch on yummy grass to stay healthy and strong. Did you know? Cows love to eat grass all day long! They have four stomachs to help them digest all that green goodness. Sometimes, the cows might walk onto land where farmers have planted their food. If cows eat the crops, the farmer will be very, very sad because their food for their family is gone. And the herder might be sad because their cows need to eat. This is another kind of problem that can happen when everyone needs the same land.

Different People, Different Ideas

Nigeria has many different groups of people, and that's amazing! Everyone has their own special traditions, songs, and ways of doing things. It's like having many different colours in a rainbow – each one is beautiful! In Plateau State, there are different groups, and sometimes they have different ideas about how to use the land or how to solve problems. When people have different ideas, it's very important to listen to each other. We should try to understand why someone wants something or feels a certain way. If we don't listen, it's harder to be friends and find a solution that makes everyone happy. We want all the different groups in Plateau State to feel like one big happy family.

When Things are Not Fair

Imagine playing a game, and you think the rules are not fair. You might feel angry or sad, right? In Plateau State, sometimes people feel that things are not fair when problems happen. They might feel like their side of the story is not being heard, or that others are getting more attention. When people feel things are not fair, it can make them even sadder or more upset. They might stop talking to each other. When people stop talking, it's really hard to solve the problems. Everyone wants to feel that they are treated with kindness and that things are fair for them and their families. We want everyone to feel safe and cared for. Did you know? Sharing and listening are like superpowers for making friends and solving problems!

Who Helps Us?

When you have a problem at home, you might ask your mum or dad for help. At school, you might ask your teacher. In a country, there are special helpers called the police or soldiers. Their job is to keep everyone safe and help solve big problems. They are called the security forces. But sometimes, people in Plateau State feel that these helpers don't always listen to them or help them fairly. When people don't trust their helpers, they might try to solve problems by themselves, and that can sometimes make things even more difficult. We need to help everyone trust the special helpers so they can do their important job of keeping everyone safe and sound.

How Can We Be Friends Again?

So, how can we make things better in Plateau State? The most important thing is to talk and listen! When people talk, they can understand each other. When they listen, they can find ways to share the land, so farmers can grow food and cows can eat grass. We need good leaders and kind people to help everyone talk and find fair ways to solve problems. It's like when your teacher helps you and your friend share a toy. Everyone needs to feel like they are part of the solution. We want people to know that when problems happen, there will be someone to help make things fair for everyone. We want everyone to live together in peace, sharing, playing, and laughing. Did you know? Nigeria has over 500 different languages! Imagine how many ways people can say 'hello' and 'thank you'!

Let's Choose Peace!

Peace is like a warm, sunny day. It means everyone is happy, safe, and can play together without worries. Even when things are tricky, like in Plateau State, we can choose peace. We can choose to be kind. We can choose to share. We can choose to listen with our hearts. It takes time and effort, like planting a seed and watching it grow into a big, strong tree. But with kindness, listening, and fair helpers, everyone in Plateau State can live together like the best of friends. Let's send our happy thoughts and wishes for peace to all the children and families in Nigeria!
summary: People in Nigeria's Plateau State sometimes have problems sharing land for farms and cows, and we need everyone to talk, listen, and trust helpers to find fair ways to live together peacefully.
whyMatters: It's important because when people have problems sharing land and don't feel things are fair, they can be sad, but if we all learn to talk, listen, and be kind, everyone can live safely and happily together like good friends.
